questions.question: Why can't a plain autoencoder generate new samples?
options: It aces reconstruction but its code space is a lumpy mess — decode a random point and you get noise; it's a compression model with no sampler
questions.explanation: Generation needs three things at once: a code space matching a clean samplable distribution, plausible decodes from any sample, and good compression — the VAE gets all three from one loss.

questions.question: What is the reparameterization trick and why is it needed?
options: Sampling z from q(z|x) isn't differentiable — rewriting the sample as z = μ + σ·ε with ε ~ N(0,I) makes z a deterministic function of (μ, σ) plus external noise, so gradients flow through the encoder
Replacing the decoder with a lookup table
questions.explanation: This single trick is why every latent-diffusion image model in 2026 has a VAE at the input — it made training stochastic encoders by backprop possible.

questions.question: What are the two terms of the VAE loss (ELBO) and how do they trade off?
options: Reconstruction (||x - x̂||²) pushes outputs toward inputs; the KL term pulls q(z|x) toward the N(0,I) prior — small β gives sharper samples but a less Gaussian code space, large β gives a cleaner code space but blurrier samples
questions.explanation: β-VAE (Higgins 2017) made this knob famous and kicked off disentanglement research. The KL penalty is precisely what forces the code space to be structured enough to sample from.

questions.question: Why does the encoder output log σ² instead of σ directly?
options: So the network's output is unconstrained — predicting σ via softplus is a trap because gradients die as σ approaches 0
To save memory on the GPU
It's an arbitrary convention with no effect
questions.explanation: A small numerical choice with real consequences: the unconstrained parameterization keeps gradients alive across the whole range of variances.

questions.question: Where do VAEs live in the 2026 generative stack?
options: They ship standalone as the top image models
Rarely standalone (outclassed by diffusion on raw quality) but as the encoder of choice for every latent-diffusion model — SD 1/2/XL/3, Flux, AudioCraft all run diffusion inside a VAE's latent space
questions.explanation: The VAE is the invisible first layer of every image pipeline you use: it compresses pixels into the latent space where diffusion is cheap, and decodes the result back.
